conditions.title: Prevention of fraud and inappropriate use of public funds

criteria.text: The provider must have in place comprehensive arrangements in relation to the higher education it plans to provide if registered (including, but not limited to, processes, policies, training and the deployment of staff and financial resources) that could reasonably be considered as being adequate and effective for the purposes of detecting, preventing and stopping any form of conduct (including a failure to act) that could potentially amount to a relevant fraud offence or the inappropriate use of relevant public funds.
criteria.threshold: Comprehensive anti-fraud arrangements that are adequate and effective for detecting, preventing and stopping fraud and inappropriate use of public funds
risk_indicators: Absence of key policies such as conflicts of interest, whistleblowing, or anti-bribery policies
Lack of dedicated staff responsible for fraud oversight and prevention
No evidence of regular fraud awareness training for staff
Missing or inadequate risk registers relating to fraud and financial misconduct

criteria.text: The provider must have a satisfactory track record in relation to receiving and/or accessing public funds.
criteria.threshold: Satisfactory track record with no convictions for fraud prevention failures, fund revocations, or similar issues within past 60 months
risk_indicators: Previous convictions under section 199 of the Economic Crime and Corporate Transparency Act 2023
Public funding access revoked or required repayment due to fraud or inappropriate use
Connected entities with history of fraud convictions or funding penalties
Ongoing investigations by funding bodies or regulatory authorities regarding financial misconduct

conditions.notes: This condition applies to all forms of higher education provision including arrangements where a provider only grants awards for students registered elsewhere. 'Arrangements' specifically includes conflicts of interest policy, internal controls, risk registers, whistleblowing policy, anti-bribery policy, staff training, oversight structures, and other necessary measures. Track record assessment covers 60 months prior to registration application date.

conditions.title: Conflicts of Interest Policy

criteria.text: "conflicts of interest policy" has the meaning given in
criteria.threshold: Institution must have a defined conflicts of interest policy with specified meaning
risk_indicators: Absence of a formally documented conflicts of interest policy
Policy exists but lacks clear definition or scope
Policy definitions are vague or inconsistent with regulatory requirements
Staff or governors are unaware of the policy's existence or requirements

conditions.notes: This appears to be a partial definition or reference to a conflicts of interest policy requirement. The full meaning and scope would typically be defined elsewhere in the regulatory framework.

conditions.title: Requirements relating to arrangements

criteria.text: Requirements relating to arrangements
criteria.threshold: Must establish and maintain appropriate arrangements as specified in the condition
risk_indicators: Lack of documented policies and procedures for handling public funds
Absence of internal controls for monitoring compliance with funding terms and conditions
No clear governance structure for oversight of public fund management
Missing or inadequate fraud prevention measures

conditions.notes: This condition includes detailed definitions for: exceptional circumstances (compelling circumstances demonstrating satisfactory track record with public funds); inappropriate use (non-compliance with funding terms, obtaining funding when ineligible); public authority (as defined in Human Rights Act 1998); relevant fraud offence (various offences under Theft Act 1968, Fraud Act 2006, and common law conspiracy to defraud); relevant person (Chancellor, Secretaries of State, OfS, UKRI, Student Loans Company, Research England, local authorities, other statutory funding bodies); and relevant public funds (money from relevant persons, including student loan funding and inter-provider payments). Applies to all providers seeking registration as an initial condition under section 5 of HERA.

conditions.title: Delivery Arrangements for Planned Higher Education Provision

criteria.text: Condition E8.2 applies to the arrangements relating to the delivery of higher education that the provider plans to offer when it is registered.
criteria.threshold: Provider must have appropriate delivery arrangements in place for all planned higher education provision upon registration
risk_indicators: Incomplete or vague delivery plans for proposed courses
Lack of adequate resources or infrastructure to support planned delivery methods
Absence of clear policies for different modes of delivery (face-to-face, distance learning, blended)
Inadequate staffing arrangements to deliver planned provision

conditions.notes: The reference to higher education provided 'in any manner or form' includes any higher education course (whether or not that course is recognised for OfS funding purposes, or any other purpose), at any level, and with any volume of learning. This means, for example, that postgraduate research courses, the study of modules or courses leading to micro credentials, and apprenticeships are included within the scope of this condition. It also includes courses provided face-to-face, by distance learning, or by a combination of delivery approaches.

conditions.title: Higher education provided by, or on behalf of, a provider

criteria.text: This includes higher education provided to all the students who are registered with a registered provider, taught by a registered provider or studying for an award of a registered provider (or where these services are provided on a registered provider's behalf).
criteria.threshold: Ensure all higher education delivery is covered regardless of registration, teaching or award arrangements
risk_indicators: Students receiving higher education services that fall outside the provider's oversight framework
Unclear accountability structures for students taught through third-party arrangements
Different quality standards applied to students based on their registration or teaching arrangements
Gaps in monitoring of educational services provided on the provider's behalf

criteria.text: This includes UK-based and non-UK-based students, and courses delivered through partnership arrangements both within the UK and internationally.
criteria.threshold: Apply consistent standards across all geographic locations and partnership arrangements
risk_indicators: Lower quality standards or monitoring for international partnerships compared to UK delivery
Non-UK students receiving different levels of educational support or oversight
Partnership agreements that lack adequate quality assurance provisions
Inconsistent application of academic standards across different geographical locations

criteria.text: The reference to 'including, but not limited to, circumstances where a provider would be responsible only for granting awards for students registered with another provider' means that a provider is required to comply with the provisions
criteria.threshold: Maintain compliance responsibilities even when only granting awards to students registered elsewhere
risk_indicators: Award-granting providers failing to verify the quality of education received by students registered elsewhere
Insufficient due diligence on the educational standards of other providers whose students receive awards
Lack of clear processes for ensuring award standards are maintained across different registration arrangements
Inadequate monitoring of academic achievement for students not directly taught by the award-granting provider

conditions.notes: Condition E8.2 establishes the broad scope of higher education provision that falls under regulatory oversight, emphasizing that providers remain responsible for quality regardless of delivery arrangements, geographic location, or partnership structures. The condition ensures comprehensive coverage of all students associated with a registered provider through registration, teaching, or award arrangements.

conditions.title: Provider role determination for awarding body status

criteria.text: where it would be the awarding body for a course, whether or not that provider would have any other role in the design or delivery of that course
criteria.threshold: Clear determination of awarding body status for courses regardless of other provider roles
risk_indicators: Ambiguity about which entity has awarding body responsibility
Confusion between teaching delivery and awarding responsibilities
Unclear documentation of provider roles in course delivery
Conflicting claims about awarding body status between partners

criteria.text: Where a provider would not be the awarding body for a course
criteria.threshold: Clear identification when provider is not the awarding body
risk_indicators: Provider claiming awarding powers they do not possess
Students unclear about who will award their qualification
Marketing materials suggesting awarding authority without proper status
Inadequate disclosure of actual awarding body to students

conditions.notes: This condition relates to the clear determination and communication of awarding body status, ensuring providers accurately represent their role in course delivery and qualification awarding.

conditions.title: Course delivery scope for partnerships and awarding body arrangements

criteria.text: Condition E8.2 applies to a course the provider itself would deliver, or which would be delivered on its behalf, regardless of the identity of the awarding body, whether or not that awarding body is registered with the OfS, or the nature of any partnership agreement.
criteria.threshold: Compliance with E8.2 requirements for all courses delivered by the provider or on its behalf
risk_indicators: Provider fails to apply E8.2 requirements to courses delivered through partnerships
Different standards applied based on awarding body registration status with OfS
Inconsistent application of requirements across different partnership arrangements
Provider assumes E8.2 requirements only apply to OfS-registered awarding bodies

conditions.notes: This condition clarifies that E8.2 requirements apply universally to all course delivery arrangements, including those with non-OfS registered awarding bodies such as Pearson, regardless of partnership structures or awarding body identity.

conditions.title: Course delivery responsibility for qualifications awarded by another provider

criteria.text: A provider applying for registration that is planning to deliver, or allow another provider to deliver, courses leading to a qualification awarded by another higher education provider, whether that awarding provider is located in England or elsewhere, is responsible for compliance with this condition in relation to those courses.
criteria.threshold: Demonstrate responsibility for compliance when delivering courses for qualifications awarded by other providers
risk_indicators: Unclear arrangements or agreements with awarding providers regarding compliance responsibilities
Lack of monitoring systems for courses delivered on behalf of other awarding providers
Evidence of non-compliance in courses where qualification is awarded by another provider
Insufficient documentation of responsibility frameworks for multi-provider arrangements

conditions.notes: This condition establishes that providers are responsible for compliance even when delivering courses leading to qualifications awarded by other higher education providers, regardless of the awarding provider's location. The provisions may result in more than one provider being responsible for compliance in multi-provider arrangements.

conditions.title: Condition E8.2 in relation to the same course

criteria.text: Condition E8.2 in relation to the same course
criteria.threshold: Compliance with condition E8.2 requirements for the specified course
risk_indicators: Failure to meet access and participation plan targets for the course
Insufficient evidence of widening participation activities
Non-compliance with student outcome measures
Inadequate monitoring of access condition requirements

conditions.notes: This appears to be a reference to condition E8.2 as it applies to a specific course. Full context and detailed requirements would typically be found in the complete condition E8.2 documentation.

conditions.title: Condition E8.2 and E8.3

criteria.text: The provider must have arrangements in place as defined in E8.3
criteria.threshold: Implementation of all required arrangements specified in E8.3
risk_indicators: Absence of documented policies and procedures
Unclear or outdated governance arrangements
Lack of evidence of arrangement implementation
Non-compliance with specific E8.3 requirements

criteria.text: E8.3 defines the arrangements that a provider must have in place for the purposes of E8.2, which includes specific arrangements as well as any other arrangements needed to comply with E8.2
criteria.threshold: Clear definition and establishment of all necessary arrangements
risk_indicators: Incomplete arrangement definitions
Missing conflicts of interest policy
Inadequate governance framework documentation
Failure to identify all required arrangements

conditions.notes: The arrangements listed in E8.3 include a conflicts of interest policy as defined in initial conditions. Document appears incomplete with reference to additional arrangement definitions.

conditions.title: Arrangements to prevent and detect fraud and inappropriate use of public funds

criteria.text: internal control processes relating to the detection, prevention and stopping of the forms of conduct set out in E8.2: structured policies, procedures, and monitoring mechanisms implemented to prevent unauthorised activities and detect indicators of fraudulent behaviour
criteria.threshold: Structured policies, procedures, and monitoring mechanisms must be implemented to prevent unauthorised activities and detect indicators of fraudulent behaviour
risk_indicators: Absence of documented fraud detection policies or procedures
No monitoring mechanisms in place to detect suspicious activities
Staff unaware of internal control processes for fraud prevention
Incidents of unauthorised activities going undetected for extended periods

criteria.text: a risk register (or equivalent document or documents) for the purpose of managing risks relating to the forms of conduct set out in E8.2: a structured document or system used to identify, assess, and manage risks within the provider, detailing each risk's likelihood, potential impact, assigned ownership, mitigation measures, and current status to support informed decision-making and compliance
criteria.threshold: A structured risk register must be maintained that identifies, assesses, and manages fraud-related risks with detailed likelihood, impact, ownership, and mitigation measures
risk_indicators: No risk register or equivalent document exists for fraud-related risks
Risk register lacks detail on likelihood, impact, or mitigation measures
Fraud-related risks not identified or assessed in business model
Risk register not regularly updated or reviewed

criteria.text: a whistleblowing policy: a policy that allows individuals to confidentially report concerns about misconduct, unethical behaviour, or regulatory breaches, while ensuring protection against retaliation and outlining investigation procedures
criteria.threshold: A whistleblowing policy must be in place that ensures confidential reporting, protection against retaliation, and clear investigation procedures
risk_indicators: No whistleblowing policy exists or policy is not accessible to staff and students
Reports of retaliation against individuals who raised concerns
Lack of confidential reporting mechanisms
Investigation procedures are unclear or not followed

criteria.text: an anti-bribery policy: a policy that outlines the provider's approach to preventing, identifying and addressing bribery
criteria.threshold: An anti-bribery policy must outline the provider's comprehensive approach to preventing, identifying and addressing bribery
risk_indicators: No anti-bribery policy in place or policy lacks detail on prevention measures
Staff unaware of what constitutes bribery or how to report it
Unclear procedures for addressing bribery allegations
Evidence of inappropriate gifts or payments involving staff

criteria.text: training for staff relating to the awareness, detection, prevention and stopping of fraud: compulsory training to ensure staff have the knowledge, skills and awareness needed to identify, prevent and report fraudulent activity
criteria.threshold: Compulsory fraud awareness training must be provided to ensure staff can identify, prevent and report fraudulent activity
risk_indicators: No fraud awareness training provided to staff or training is not compulsory
Training not tailored to staff roles and responsibilities
Training not regularly reviewed and updated
Staff demonstrate lack of knowledge about fraud detection and reporting

criteria.text: deployment of staff with responsibility for overseeing the policies, processes, training and other arrangements put in place for the purposes set out in E8.2, and structures for oversight of those staff and: the deployment of staff with defined responsibilities for the arrangements listed in E8.3 for the purposes set out in E8.2 to ensure that internal controls are effectively applied and risks are actively managed
criteria.threshold: Staff must be deployed with defined responsibilities for overseeing fraud prevention arrangements, with appropriate oversight structures in place
risk_indicators: No designated staff responsible for fraud prevention oversight
Unclear responsibilities or accountability for fraud prevention arrangements
Lack of oversight structures for staff managing fraud prevention
Staff responsible for fraud prevention lack necessary authority or resources

criteria.text: any other arrangements necessary for the purposes of complying with E8.2: this may include, but is not limited to, internal and external audit functions and integration with broader governance systems
criteria.threshold: Additional arrangements necessary for compliance must be in place, including internal/external audit functions and integration with governance systems
risk_indicators: Absence of internal audit function or inadequate external audit arrangements
Fraud prevention arrangements not integrated with broader governance systems
Gaps in arrangements that leave provider vulnerable to fraud risks
Arrangements operate in isolation without coherent integration

criteria.text: In order for a provider to comply with E8.2, it must have comprehensive arrangements in place that could reasonably be considered to be adequate and effective for the purposes of the condition
criteria.threshold: Comprehensive arrangements must be in place that are adequate and effective for preventing fraud and inappropriate use of public funds
risk_indicators: Written policies exist but operational arrangements are not implemented
Arrangements are preventative but lack proactive detection capabilities
Arrangements conflict with one another or fail to align
Arrangements are not appropriate to the provider's context or adequately resourced

conditions.notes: Comprehensive arrangements must be broad in scope, covering various fraud scenarios and inappropriate use of public funds. Arrangements must be adequate (delivering stated objectives, sufficient scope, appropriately resourced) and effective (preventative and responsive, regularly reviewed, with appropriate oversight). Providers must assess their own context and risks to determine appropriate arrangements. Examples include robust registration processes, quality monitoring, data validation controls, transparent financial procedures, and designated compliance officers where appropriate.

conditions.title: Satisfactory track record in relation to receiving and/or accessing public funds

criteria.text: The provider must have a satisfactory track record in relation to receiving and/or accessing public funds. This requirement is not restricted to convictions or decisions relating to higher education provision or to education and training.
criteria.threshold: Demonstrated satisfactory history of managing public funds without significant breaches or convictions
risk_indicators: Previous convictions for fraud offences involving public funds
Final decisions by government bodies to cease funding payments
Required repayments of previously received public funds
Suspension or revocation of access to Student Loans Company funding

conditions.notes: Receiving public funds includes payments from Student Loans Company for tuition fees, maintenance payments, or OfS funding. Accessing public funds includes receiving tuition fee payments through lead providers. Final decisions exclude provisional decisions or temporary suspensions. Direct and indirect revocations of funding access are both considered. Repayments may be direct or through offset against future payments.

conditions.title: Comprehensive, adequate and effective arrangements for preventing fraud and inappropriate use of public funds

criteria.text: A provider must submit a full and complete account of any exceptional circumstances it wishes the OfS to consider if any circumstances set out in paragraph E8.4 apply to the provider.
criteria.threshold: Full disclosure and documentation of exceptional circumstances
risk_indicators: Provider fails to disclose known issues with public fund management
Incomplete or delayed submission of exceptional circumstances documentation
Evidence of withholding material information about funding irregularities
Pattern of late or amended submissions regarding financial circumstances

criteria.text: A provider must have comprehensive, adequate, and effective arrangements for preventing fraud or the inappropriate use of public funds.
criteria.threshold: Demonstrable systems and controls to prevent fraud and misuse of public funding
risk_indicators: Absence of documented fraud prevention policies and procedures
Weak internal controls over financial management and reporting
Lack of segregation of duties in financial processes
No regular monitoring or audit of public fund usage

criteria.text: Inappropriate use is defined to include but is not limited to: Non-compliance with legally binding terms and conditions that specify or restrict how funding can be used; Obtaining or continuing to receive funding in circumstances where any conditions or criteria that determine a recipient's eligibility to receive the funding are not satisfied.
criteria.threshold: Compliance with all funding terms, conditions and eligibility criteria
risk_indicators: Use of restricted funding for unauthorized purposes
Submission of incorrect student data leading to overpayment
Continuing to claim funding despite changed eligibility status
Material differences between forecast and actual student numbers beyond normal tolerance

conditions.notes: This condition covers arrangements for preventing fraud and inappropriate use of public funds. The OfS will not consider intention to prevent fraud as an exceptional circumstance after a final decision has been made. Relevant public funds include any funding paid in relation to students taught by the provider, whether paid directly or indirectly. Assessment considers governing body documents, business plans, and submitted arrangements for preventing fraud.

conditions.title: Financial viability and sustainability

criteria.text: When assessing a provider's arrangements for the purposes of E8.2, the OfS will consider whether the provider has policies and processes in place to adequately and effectively manage risks relating to fraud and the inappropriate use of public funding identified within its business plan.
criteria.threshold: Provider must have adequate and effective policies and processes to manage fraud and inappropriate use of public funding risks
risk_indicators: Business plan identifies high-risk activities (third-party agents, partnerships) without corresponding risk management frameworks
Absence of documented fraud prevention policies or procedures
Previous history of financial irregularities or public funding misuse
Weak internal controls around financial processes and oversight

criteria.text: In assessing whether a provider has comprehensive, adequate and effective arrangements for detecting, preventing or stopping fraud or the inappropriate use of public funds, the OfS's judgement will apply to the arrangements the provider will have in place if it is registered.
criteria.threshold: Provider must demonstrate comprehensive, adequate and effective arrangements for detecting, preventing or stopping fraud or inappropriate use of public funds
risk_indicators: Inadequate fraud detection systems or monitoring procedures
Limited segregation of duties in financial operations
Poor oversight of subcontractual arrangements or partnerships involving public funds
Failure to report or investigate suspected fraud incidents

criteria.text: The OfS will draw on any relevant evidence or intelligence available to it, including information obtained through its own regulatory activity, third party notifications, or publicly available information, including action taken by other regulators, or media reporting.
criteria.threshold: Provider must not have adverse evidence of fraud or inappropriate use of public funds from regulatory, third party, or public sources
risk_indicators: Adverse media reports about financial misconduct or fraud
Regulatory action by other bodies relating to financial irregularities
Third-party complaints or whistleblower reports about financial practices
Public records showing financial penalties or sanctions

criteria.text: A provider will be required to submit a declaration as part of its application for registration confirming whether any of the circumstances in E8.4.a apply. A provider must ensure that the information submitted in relation to this requirement is accurate and complete.
criteria.threshold: Provider must submit accurate and complete declarations regarding circumstances that may affect financial integrity
risk_indicators: Incomplete or missing declarations in registration application
Inconsistencies between declared information and available evidence
Late amendments to declarations after submission
Reluctance to provide additional information when requested

conditions.notes: The OfS assessment considers arrangements that will be in place if registered, but may be informed by previous conduct relating to inappropriate use of public funds or fraud offences. This applies to providers with previous experience in higher education, further education, or other education/training delivery. The OfS may undertake further investigation where concerns exist based on available information.
